image_headline: FOUR PRICES THAT KILL GSA MARGINS
image_prompt: Infographic numbered breakdown layout. Cream background. Bold headline top: 'FOUR PRICES THAT KILL GSA MARGINS'. Left column: illustrated professional man in blazer reviewing a pricing spreadsheet on a laptop, expression focused. Right column: four numbered rows (01, 02, 03, 04), each with a short 2-3 word label — '01 WRONG FLOOR', '02 MFC BLIND SPOT', '03 OVERHEAD OVERLOAD', '04 NO EPA TERMS' — each row has a small red warning icon. Bottom dark navy banner: 'PRICE IT RIGHT FIRST'. Footer text: 'Josh Ladick · GSA Focus'.

template_data.headline_html: Four numbers that silently kill your GSA margins.

items.title: Wrong commercial rate as your floor
items.desc: Your Basis of Award price is a compliance anchor. Routinely discounting below it for commercial clients creates a problem you'll find at modification time.

items.title: Ignoring the Most Favored Customer rule
items.desc: GSA wants parity with your best customer. If you don't know who that is or what you charge them, you're negotiating blind.

items.title: Loading overhead with no negotiating room
items.desc: Contracting Officers expect to push back. Build space into your proposal — not to give it away, but because the CO needs to win something.

items.title: No Economic Price Adjustment terms
items.desc: EPA provisions let you update prices over time. Skip negotiating them upfront and you're locked in regardless of cost changes.
